description: Fix E2E test reliability issues in plugin-logging, analyze-site, secret-rotation, and config-watcher test files: weak assertions, misleading failure messages, tab leaks, and a race condition in log truncation. Also add missing E2E coverage for stale MCP session error path.

userStories.title: Strengthen plugin-logging.e2e.ts log buffer count assertion
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the 'increments log buffer size by exactly the number of entries emitted' test to detect overcounting bugs so that duplicate log entry emission is caught by tests.
acceptanceCriteria: The assertion at line ~121 uses toBe(bufferBefore + 4) instead of toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(bufferBefore + 4)

If flaky due to parallel test pollution (other tests emitting logs to the same plugin's buffer), add an upper bound check: expect(bufferAfter - bufferBefore).toBeLessThanOrEqual(5) to allow at most 1 extra entry from concurrent activity
The test title accurately reflects what it verifies

userStories.title: Add assertion guard for undefined analyzeSiteServer in analyze-site.e2e.ts
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want analyze-site tests to fail immediately with a clear error message when the shared test server fails to start so that debugging is not obscured by misleading connection errors.
acceptanceCriteria: The analyzeSite helper function at line ~96 asserts that analyzeSiteServer is defined at the top of the function (before using its URL), throwing a clear error like 'analyzeSiteServer not initialized — beforeAll may have failed'
The `analyzeSiteServer?.url ?? ''` fallback at line ~143 in the test body is also replaced with an assertion or non-null access
If beforeAll fails to start the server, subsequent tests fail immediately with the assertion error rather than with misleading 'connection refused to empty URL' errors
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/analyze-site.e2e.ts

The module-level `let analyzeSiteServer: TestServer | undefined` is set in beforeAll (line 125-127). If startAnalyzeSiteServer() throws, it stays undefined. Each test uses `analyzeSiteServer?.url ?? ''` (line 143) which silently produces an empty string URL.

Fix in two places:
1. In the analyzeSite helper (line 96), add at the top:
   if (!analyzeSiteServer) throw new Error('analyzeSiteServer not initialized — beforeAll may have failed');
2. In each test body, replace `analyzeSiteServer?.url ?? ''` with `analyzeSiteServer!.url` (safe because the helper already guards it), or better yet, extract the URL inside the helper from the parameter.

Actually, the cleanest fix: add the guard in the analyzeSite helper and change the test bodies to pass the URL directly. But looking at the code, the analyzeSite helper already receives `url` as a parameter — the `analyzeSiteServer?.url ?? ''` is in the test body constructing the URL. So fix in the test body: replace `analyzeSiteServer?.url ?? ''` with a local `const baseUrl = analyzeSiteServer?.url; if (!baseUrl) throw new Error(...);` or just assert and use `analyzeSiteServer!.url`.

userStories.title: Add try/finally for tab cleanup in analyze-site.e2e.ts analyzeSite helper
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the analyzeSite helper to clean up analysis tabs even when the tool call throws so that browser tabs don't accumulate across the 11+ analyze-site tests.
acceptanceCriteria: The analyzeSite helper at lines 96-116 wraps the callTool and tab cleanup in try/finally so that tab cleanup runs even when the tool call throws
The tab snapshot (browser_list_tabs before) is taken before the try block
The tab cleanup (closing new tabs) is in the finally block
The tool result is still returned correctly on success
On failure, the original error is re-thrown after cleanup

userStories.title: Fix confusing error pattern in secret-rotation.e2e.ts old-secret rejection test
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the secret rotation test to clearly report when the old secret is incorrectly accepted so that genuine bugs produce clear failure messages instead of confusing assertion errors.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'old secret is rejected' test at lines 163-174 no longer throws into its own catch block
If initialize() succeeds with the old secret (a genuine bug), the test fails with a clear message like 'Old secret should have been rejected but initialize() succeeded'
If initialize() fails with the expected 401/Unauthorized error, the test passes
If initialize() fails with an unexpected error (not 401), the test fails with the actual error message

userStories.title: Fix race in log truncation before waitForLog in config-watcher.e2e.ts
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the config-watcher hot reload test to track new log occurrences instead of truncating logs so that log lines emitted during the truncation window are not lost.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'config watcher still works after hot reload' test at line ~223 no longer uses server.logs.length = 0 to truncate logs before waitForLog

Instead, it captures the current log count before the action and uses a helper or inline logic to wait for a NEW occurrence of the expected log substring (similar to the writeAndWaitForWatcher pattern in helpers.ts which counts occurrences before/after)
The test correctly detects 'Hot reload complete' and 'Config watcher: Watching' after the hot reload

userStories.title: Add E2E test for stale MCP session ID error response
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want an E2E test that verifies the MCP server returns a 400 'missing session' error when a client sends a request with a stale or fabricated session ID so that session lifecycle error handling is validated end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test is added to e2e/dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts (or a relevant existing E2E file) that verifies the stale session error path
The test creates an MCP client, initializes a session, then manually sends a request with a fabricated or invalidated mcp-session-id header
The server responds with HTTP 400 and the response body contains 'missing session' or equivalent error message
The test verifies the error response status code and message
